title: Disaster Related Case Management - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Disaster Services > Disaster Related Case Management 8 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ACCESS CENTER FOR COMMUNITY RESILIENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports those affected by wildfire, including transitioning fire survivors into affordable and permanent housing. Housing case management services look different for each household, but may include: -Advocating for fire-impacted households to help find stable, safe, and affordable housing -Identifying short- and long-term housing needs -Developing individual housing stability plans -Helping program participants conduct housing searches -Providing participants with rent payment and utility bill assistance -Homebuyer counseling services -Homeowner and tenant education opportunities -Collaboration with local partner agencies to provide comprehensive support including disaster relief, mental health, and substance abuse case management 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Individuals and families impacted by 2020 Jackson County wildfires Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 414-0319 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS CRISIS AND EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates with local, state, and federal agencies to facilitate emergency preparedness training and community education and outreach. Provides wraparound social services, prevention kits, and emergency food and financial assistance. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Varies by service. Emergency financial assistance must be directly impacted by COVID-19. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-3119 110 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES ODHS DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF This program is designed to help survivors of specific natural disaster events connect to ODHS Disaster Case Managers. Intake does not guarantee assistance. Disaster case workers may be able to assist individuals in identifying and applying for disaster recovery support or addressing urgent needs. Services will vary based on eligibility and need. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Residents of Oregon who have been affected by natural disasters Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 669-0554 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE FOOD UNITES DISASTER CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and resource navigation services for those affected by the 2020 Labor Day wildfires.
